purpose: Source-backed literal architecture specs for generated WAM diagrams. Each spec should be grounded in method-section extracts, not only survey-level taxonomy.

curationStatus.note: Only models in completeEnoughForRendering may render connected architecture graphs. All other cards show evidence notes without inferred topology until their paper is reviewed at node-and-edge level.

inputTokens: task language encoded by Wan2.2 built-in T5 text encoder
clean latent tokens of the first observation frame from the pretrained video VAE
noisy future-video latent tokens used only during training
action chunk tokens processed by an action expert DiT
tokenization: visual observations -> pretrained Wan2.2 video VAE latent tokens
language -> T5 embeddings supplied to all token groups through cross-attention
actions -> action encoder tokens for chunk horizon h=32
backbone: Wan2.2-5B video Diffusion Transformer used as single-pass world encoder
Mixture-of-Transformer layout with shared attention between video and action branches
1B action expert DiT with reduced hidden dimension da=1024
branches: video branch denoises future video latents during training
action branch denoises action tokens
future video branch is removed entirely at inference
attention: all token groups cross-attend to language embeddings
future video tokens attend bidirectionally within video branch and to clean first-frame tokens
action tokens attend bidirectionally within action branch and to clean first-frame tokens
action tokens cannot attend to future video tokens
clean first-frame tokens do not attend to other tokens
heads: action expert DiT predicts action chunk velocity field
video DiT predicts future latent-frame velocity field during co-training
objectives: flow matching over action chunk a_1:H
flow matching over future video latents z_1:T
joint loss L = L_act + lambda L_vid
trainingRecipe: train with clean first-frame tokens, noisy future video latents, and noisy action tokens
use structured mask to prevent future-video leakage into action branch
same flow-matching formulation for video and action branches
inferenceRecipe: keep only clean first-frame latent tokens
run video DiT once to produce latent world representation
denoise action tokens with action expert
do not instantiate future video tokens or perform future video denoising

inputTokens: future RGB/video latents, future state latents, and action latents during denoising
tokenization: RGB videos -> original Wan2.2 causal VAE encoder E
proprioceptive states -> learnable MLP_s latent projection
actions -> learnable MLP_a latent projection
depth maps -> pseudo-RGB replicated channels encoded in same VAE latent space
view embeddings mark camera index; temporal RoPE aligns state/action/video time
backbone: Wan2.2-TI2V-5B video generation Diffusion Transformer
unified denoising sequence Z = [zO0, zO1:H, zs0, zs1:H, za1:K]
bidirectional full attention over unified denoising sequence
branches: main RGB/state/action branch
auxiliary depth branch made by replicating final M DiT blocks
depth branch reads main branch through unilateral cross-attention
attention: depth branch attends to same-layer main branch input
main branch is unaffected by depth tokens
initial observation and state remain clean with timestep t=0
heads: VAE decoder reconstructs RGB and depth
symmetric MLP decoders recover state and action latents
action output is available after Ta denoising steps
objectives: flow matching velocity prediction for O, s, and a modalities
depth branch inverse-depth MSE loss
coupled asynchronous noise-level sampling with tO >= ta
trainingRecipe: fine-tune from Wan2.2-5B with multi-view RGB, states, actions, and depth supervision
sample video/action noise from a joint distribution that matches asynchronous inference
predict H=8 future RGB frames, H=8 future states, and K=32 actions
inferenceRecipe: start video and action from noise
run Ta action/state denoising steps and dispatch actions
optionally continue video denoising to TO steps for high-fidelity RGB-D future
depth branch can be toggled to reduce rollout overhead

diagramModules.label: ANS Sampler
diagramModules.details: Ta action steps, TO video steps

diagramModules.label: Actions + Future State
diagramModules.details: dispatch actions after Ta; predict s1:H

inputTokens: DINOv2 patch features O_t and O_t+k
third-view RGB and language for policy
tokenization: DINOv2 converts video frames to object-centric patch features
spatial-temporal transformer IDM encoder infers latent action tokens
VQ codebooks quantize task-irrelevant and task-centric latent actions
Prismatic-7B VLM vocabulary is extended with ACT_1..ACT_C tokens
backbone: two-stage latent action model with IDM encoder and FDM decoder
Prismatic-7B policy: SigLIP + DINOv2 visual encoder, projection layer, LLaMA-2 LLM
auto-regressive latent action prediction
branches: Stage 1 learns task-irrelevant action code conditioned on language
Stage 2 freezes task-irrelevant codebook and learns task-centric codebook
post-training action decoder maps latent actions to low-level controls
attention: IDM encoder is a spatial-temporal transformer with causal temporal masks
post-training decoder uses multi-head attention pooling over visual and latent action embeddings
heads: FDM spatial transformer predicts future DINOv2 features
auto-regressive VLM predicts next latent action token
lightweight action decoder projects latent action embeddings to embodiment-specific action chunks
objectives: DINOv2 feature reconstruction ||O_hat_t+k - O_t+k||^2
VQ-VAE objective for latent action quantization
next-latent-action negative log likelihood
post-training L1 loss on low-level actions
trainingRecipe: Stage 1: learn task-irrelevant latent action with T5 instruction conditioning
Stage 2: copy/freeze Stage 1 codebook and learn new task-centric latent action codebook
pretrain generalist policy by labeling videos with latent action tokens
post-train with LoRA and a small action head for target embodiment
inferenceRecipe: policy receives RGB, task instruction, and optional history latent actions
auto-regressively predicts N=4 latent action tokens
action decoder converts latent action embedding plus visual context into low-level action chunk

diagramModules.label: Latent-action Labels
diagramModules.details: ACT targets for generalist policy pretraining

tokenization: SigLIP-2 vision/text encoders produce image and instruction tokens
four self-attention transformer layers fuse vision-language tokens
Q-former compresses fused sequence into M=32 query tokens
state encoder and action encoder feed the DiT policy sequence
backbone: Diffusion Transformer policy with alternating cross-attention and self-attention layers
future tokens interact with action stream through DiT self-attention
action-aware future embedding model is pretrained with 8 DiT blocks
branches: action flow-matching branch predicts denoising direction for action chunk
future-token branch is sliced from internal DiT layer L and aligned to future observation embedding
attention: DiT alternates self-attention and cross-attention to condition on robot vision-language embedding
learnable future tokens and action stream interact through self-attention
heads: MLP projection maps future-token activations to teacher embedding dimension
action denoising head outputs flow velocity for action chunk
objectives: flow matching loss L_fm for action chunk
future latent alignment loss using cosine similarity
combined loss L = L_fm + lambda L_align with lambda=0.2
trainingRecipe: pretrain compact action-aware embedding model on about 2,000 hours of cross-embodiment robot data
post-train policy jointly with action flow matching and future latent alignment
EMA update adapts embedding target with policy encoder during finetuning
inferenceRecipe: sample initial action chunk noise
perform K=4 forward Euler denoising steps
future tokens shape hidden state but no future pixels are decoded

diagramModules.label: Future Tokens
diagramModules.details: M learnable slots at layer L

inputTokens: frozen Eagle-2 VLM features Phi_t from current observation and task instruction
tokenization: Eagle-2 layer-12 semantic features condition diffusion module
future image embedding target comes from SIGLIP-2 representations produced by Eagle-2
each image yields 256 tokens, reduced to 64 tokens via 2x2 average pooling
diffusion module processes 1 state token, 16 action tokens, and 64 future image tokens
backbone: 12 MMDiT blocks for joint-modal processing
4 modality-specific DiT blocks for action stream
4 modality-specific DiT blocks for vision stream
branches: action stream denoises low-level motor trajectories
vision stream denoises future image embedding
streams concatenate only temporarily inside shared cross-modal attention
attention: separate action and vision pathways inside each MMDiT block
shared cross-modal attention exchanges information
streams split back for other operations
interleaved VLM conditioning with alternating self-attention and cross-attention
heads: action velocity prediction head V_theta^A
future embedding velocity prediction head V_theta^o
objectives: independent per-modality noising for actions and future image embeddings
flow matching action loss L_A
world-modeling embedding loss L_WM
joint loss L_joint = L_A + lambda_WM L_WM
trainingRecipe: frozen VLM backbone; diffusion expert randomly initialized
independently sample tau_A and tau_o to learn forward and inverse dependencies
world-model target avoids direct pixel prediction
inferenceRecipe: jointly sample actions and future embeddings in parallel
vision tokens can update every fine step while action tokens update every q steps
q controls test-time speed/accuracy scaling

inputTokens: language instruction tokens supplied through cross-attention
tokenization: three camera views merged into one composite image at standard resolution
current and sparse future composite observations encoded with pretrained VAE
proprioceptive states and actions linearly projected to hidden dimension
visual tokens use 2D positional encoding; state/action tokens use 1D temporal positional encoding
backbone: 5B Wan diffusion Transformer adapted with action-centered objective
single shared stack of Transformer blocks, not MoE
all token types share Q/K/V projection matrices at every layer
branches: action branch reads current observations and states
future-video branch reads observations, states, and action chunks
language instruction is external cross-attention condition
attention: blockwise causal mask: state and current observation attend to each other
action tokens attend to state/current observation but not future video
future-video tokens attend to state/current observation/action
prevents future-frame leakage into action generation
heads: action flow head predicts action-token velocity field
video flow head predicts future VAE-latent velocity field
objectives: pretraining optimizes only video flow-matching objective
post-training combines video and action flow objectives with scalar weights
trainingRecipe: initialize from Wan pretrained video model
embodied pretraining on about 10,000 hours of robot-centered and egocentric human video
post-train on target-robot trajectories pairing images, language, and actions
inferenceRecipe: drop future visual tokens from deployed action path
generate action tokens from current observation/state/language
future video can be modeled as feedforward dynamics conditioned on action chunks when needed

inputTokens: separate diffusion timesteps t_a and t_o'
tokenization: current observations encoded by ResNet-18 conditioner
future observations encoded into SDXL VAE latents and patchified
actions encoded into tokens with shallow MLP
diffusion timesteps encoded with shared sinusoidal timestep encoder
backbone: diffusion Transformer with observation and timestep conditioning via AdaLN
register tokens provide intermediary medium for action and image modalities
implementation uses 12 layers, embed dim 768, 12 heads, action length 16
branches: same model can act as policy, video predictor, inverse dynamics, or forward dynamics by setting modality timesteps
robot trajectory batches train both action and next-observation prediction
action-free video batches mask out actions via diffusion timestep
attention: tokens share Transformer context with register tokens
conditioning is through AdaLN rather than cross-attention in the main implementation
heads: action noise prediction head
future observation latent noise prediction head
VAE decoder can decode future image latents
objectives: standard diffusion denoising loss across action and next-observation noise predictions
independent modality-specific timesteps enable marginalization/conditioning
trainingRecipe: sample transition tuples (o, a, o')
sample action and observation diffusion timesteps independently
co-train with action-free video by setting action diffusion timestep to fully noisy/masked
inferenceRecipe: policy: set image diffusion timestep to T and denoise actions
inverse dynamics: condition on clean future observation and denoise actions
forward dynamics: condition on action and denoise future observations
video prediction: denoise future observation marginal

inputTokens: language instruction tokenized by frozen text encoder
sequence of video frames tokenized by VQGAN
multi-view robot image sequences during finetuning
robot state sequence containing end-effector/gripper state
tokenization: VQGAN converts each image into discrete tokens and is frozen
frozen text encoder tokenizes instruction
robot states are embedded as sequence inputs
backbone: GPT-style transformer visual manipulation policy
pretrained on text-video next future image token prediction
fine-tuned for future image generation and action trajectory prediction
branches: video generation branch predicts future image tokens for each view
cVAE action trajectory branch generates Cartesian action chunks
attention: GPT-style causal sequence modeling over text, image, state, future image, and action-related tokens
heads: future image token output decoded with VQGAN decoder
conditional VAE action trajectory decoder
whole-body controller converts Cartesian trajectory to low-level joint actions
objectives: video generative pretraining on 38 million clips / about 50B tokens
robot finetuning for future images and action trajectory
trainingRecipe: Stage 1: large-scale video-language pretraining predicts future images
Stage 2: robot data finetuning accepts multi-view frames, state sequence, and predicts future views plus action trajectory
inferenceRecipe: condition on language, image history, and robot state
predict future image tokens and cVAE action trajectory
execute Cartesian trajectory via WBC to joint commands

inputTokens: static and wrist predictive feature maps
tokenization: SVD-based text-guided video prediction model encodes future visual sequence
first denoising/forward step exposes latent predictive features
features from selected up-sampling layer are resized and stacked
Video Former aggregates high-dimensional frame features into fixed tokens
backbone: Stable Video Diffusion foundation model adapted as text-guided video predictor
Video Former feature aggregator
DiT diffusion policy action head
branches: video predictor branch produces hidden predictive representations
action policy branch consumes aggregated Video Former tokens
attention: video model uses language feature through cross-attention
Video Former uses learnable tokens with spatial attention over static/wrist predictive features
policy DiT uses cross-attention to aggregated predictive representation
heads: Video Former aggregation head outputs Q''
diffusion Transformer policy denoises action sequence
objectives: video diffusion objective reconstructs future visual sequence
diffusion policy objective denoises action sequence conditioned on predictive representation
trainingRecipe: Stage 1: fine-tune video diffusion model on internet human and robot manipulation data
Stage 2: train generalist diffusion policy from Video Former predictive tokens
inferenceRecipe: run video diffusion model for only a single forward step
extract selected hidden features instead of decoding video
aggregate through Video Former and denoise actions
